name: anomaly_detection_v1
description: Anomaly detection training dataset for adapterOS metrics. 600 examples covering the five primary anomaly patterns: latency spikes, error rate surges, memory leak signatures, routing collapse, and heartbeat storms. Each example presents observable metric patterns and expects identification, root cause analysis, and remediation steps. Includes both true anomaly (positive) and false alarm (negative) examples.

entries.notes: 600 anomaly detection examples across 5 subcategories. 392 positive (true anomaly) and 208 negative (false alarm) examples.

latency_spikes.example_threshold: if baseline is 200ms, anomaly fires at >400ms

error_rate_surges.definition: Error count jumping >5x in a 1-minute window
error_rate_surges.example_threshold: if baseline is 0.2%, anomaly fires at >1.0%

memory_leak_patterns.definition: Monotonically increasing memory usage without corresponding adapter loads
memory_leak_patterns.key_signal: linear growth over hours regardless of traffic variation

routing_collapse.definition: Router entropy drops to near-zero with one adapter handling >95% of traffic

heartbeat_storms.definition: Rapid connect/disconnect cycles exceeding 10 state transitions per minute per adapter

provenance.review_notes: Handcrafted examples with realistic metric values, adapterOS-specific terminology, and actionable remediation steps. False alarm examples cover common misidentification patterns.

evaluation_gates: True positive rate >= 90% on held-out anomaly scenarios
False positive rate <= 15% on held-out normal pattern scenarios
Remediation step completeness >= 80% (covers immediate action + follow-up)
